headline.subheadline: Cuts pain relief costs up to 90% - Relieves pain from arthritis, back, bursitis, carpal tunnel, tendonitis, sprains, strains and more

schema_analysis.existing_schema: Pain relief requires either drugs (dangerous) or slow topical creams
schema_analysis.new_schema: A 71-year-old Japanese patch delivers safe, fast relief directly to pain source
schema_analysis.bridge_mechanism: 71-year safety record + Oriental medicine origin + direct-to-source delivery + COX-2 contrast

headline.vsl_adaptation: While Americans have been taking dangerous drugs that cause heart attacks and strokes, the Japanese have been using a simple patch to relieve pain in just 10 minutes... for 71 years. Now it's finally available in the US...

lead.opening_verbatim: A product that relieves pain in 10 minutes without taking dangerous drugs has been used safely in Japan for 71 years. It is now available in the United States. This product is a patch that contains ingredients that not only relieve pain quickly, but, also help remove toxins from the damaged area.
lead.transition_to_body: It can cut your pain relief costs up to 90% less than taking pain relief drugs.

mechanism.mechanism_description: The patch contains Oriental medicine ingredients in advanced forms of fiber. When applied, ingredients are released over time through the skin deeply into muscles, bones, and joints. The ingredients first cool and reduce inflammation, then gently warm the area, causing vasodilation (blood vessel expansion). This increased blood flow delivers oxygen and nutrients while carrying away toxins. Unlike oral drugs, the patch bypasses the liver and concentrates relief at the pain source.
mechanism.safety_positioning: 71-year safety record - bypasses liver to avoid side effects

proof_elements.verbatim: has been used safely in Japan for 71 years
brought to market commercially by a Japanese pharmaceutical company in 1934
cut your pain relief costs up to 90% less than taking pain relief drugs
as you have seen in media reports and the recall of a number of these synthetic pain relief drugs

key_sections.content_verbatim: Its origin is in ancient Oriental medicine. It is now well known to science that there are many remedies from Oriental medicine are now proving to be very effective in clinical studies. This particular Oriental pain relief method was brought to market commercially by a Japanese pharmaceutical company in 1934.

Going directly to the pain source also has this major health benefit. You are not putting pain relief substances all through your body in places where they are not needed, but, rather you concentrate the substances at the point of the pain. Also, you are not putting pain relief substances through the liver, which is one of the main causes of side effects of ingestible pain relief remedies.

The main mechanism of action of these synthetic drugs is COX-2 Inhibition. The COX-2 enzyme causes inflammation by stimulating the bad prostaglandins... But, these synthetic drugs can produce bad side effects because the COX-2 enzyme is also necessary for cardiovascular health.

testimonials.quote: I honestly did not expect much in the way of results, because I had already tried sports creams and also an elbow sleeve that has a sports cream in the sleeve. Neither worked at all. That's why I was surprised when the PainNOT patch actually took the pain away!

The doctor told me that I needed surgery for my back but I did not want to go through that. Now when I feel pain in my back I use the PainNOT Patch. In 10 minutes I start experiencing movement in my back again and even the next day my back feels wonderful.
I wear the PainNOT patch when I play golf, and it stays on very well, especially as my skin gets warmer.

narrative_flow.interviewee: John Whitakre, Chief of Staff, Product Development, Bio Tech Research

offer.limit: Strict limit of 3 boxes at discount price

extraction_notes.key_insight: Origin marketing works best when combined with a strong countersell - the Japanese 71-year safety record directly contrasts with American drugs being recalled for causing heart attacks
